taskCaption: item starts near the drawer and is placed inside it (relation-event diagnostic)

pilotMetadata.tests: Asserts, via the FROZEN matcher: the item's TERMINAL relation to the container is INSIDE (hard OBJECT_RELATION_GOAL -> goal_satisfaction); the item STARTED NEAR, not already inside (initial_state, from the authored r0 NEAR endpoint); it ENDED INSIDE (terminal_state + relation_transitions); and one CONTAINMENT_CHANGE event is present (event_presence). One structural step beyond terminal-only: it requires the item to have STARTED near and ACHIEVED INSIDE, not merely to be inside at the end. No contact, gripper-grasp, co-motion, release, or temporal order.
addedOverTerminalOnly: relations(authored NEAR r0 + INSIDE r1) -> contributes initial_state(NEAR) + terminal_state(INSIDE)
pilotMetadata.enforcedVsAuthoring: CRITICAL: a 'born-inside' episode is rejected by initial_state, NOT by relation_transitions/event_presence. csg/rollout_extract.py seeds prev_rel='NEAR' UNCONDITIONALLY, so an item that ends INSIDE -- even one inside the whole time -- ALWAYS emits a NEAR->INSIDE CONTAINMENT_CHANGE delta. The only thing distinguishing a real put-in from born-inside is the robot's FIRST relation fact (NEAR for a real put-in, INSIDE for born-inside), caught by initial_state. ENFORCED: terminal INSIDE, INSIDE achieved (relation_transitions), initial NEAR endpoint (initial_state), CONTAINMENT_CHANGE present (event_presence). event_order stays support 0 (one event, no pair to order against). AUTHORING ONLY (not verifier-enforced): timeSpan magnitudes, confidences, geometry source/category, representative object sizes.
pilotMetadata.deferredNote: Asserting gripper-contact or causal order from the recorded effector trace is deferred to a later target until it can be honestly evidenced without weakening leakage discipline. The single CONTAINMENT_CHANGE event carries event_presence + relation_transitions but ZERO event-order pair support (no second event), so event_order stays vacuous.
pilotMetadata.source: RLBench PutItemInDrawer (bottom/middle/top variations); external simulation leg of the object_inside_container flagship task. Live demos recorded on Runpod/CoppeliaSim by pilots.rlbench.record_put_item_in_drawer; raw sim dumps stay off git. Object sizes here are representative; the container volume in each rollout is the success proximity sensor's bounding box.
pilotMetadata.notAGoldTask: Pilot diagnostic, deliberately NOT added to gold_tests/. It asserts the item started near and was placed inside the drawer; it does NOT assert the agent contacted or caused the motion. Read it for relation-event mappability from an external simulator, not as a full object_inside_container acceptance test.
